mcq.question: What is a packet?
mcq.correct: A small piece of data sent across a network
wrong: A set of rules for sending data
A device used to connect networks

mcq.question: Why is data split into packets?
mcq.correct: To allow efficient and reliable transmission
wrong: To hide the contents of the data

mcq.question: Which of the following is NOT part of a packet?
What is stored in the payload?
What is the role of the header?
wrong: Mark the end of the packet

mcq.question: Which of the following is found in the header?
What is the function of the trailer?
mcq.correct: Marks the end and checks for errors

mcq.question: What is packet switching?
mcq.correct: Sending packets independently and reassembling them
wrong: Sending the whole message in one block

mcq.question: What is the first step in packet switching?
wrong: Packets are put back in order

mcq.question: What device is used to send packets between networks?
Why might packets take different routes?
mcq.correct: Routers choose the best available route
wrong: To stop the packets being checked

mcq.question: Why might packets arrive out of order?
wrong: They are all sent at the same speed
They are stored in the payload
They are checked by the trailer

mcq.question: How are packets put back in the correct order?
mcq.correct: Using the packet number in the header

mcq.question: What extra information helps reassemble a message?
